Market Context: NEUTRAL — Briefing called neutral, day played out as mild bearish. VIX rose into high vol territory (>25) by afternoon. Iran peace announcement pending overnight.
Lessons Learned: Catalyst trade. Took position knowing it would expire BEFORE Trump Iran peace announcement (8 hours away). Underlying ~$652 at entry, short strike $658, ~10% ITM probability. SPY moved into ITM by ~$1.30 in the final minutes (4pm ET close). 100% premium stop did NOT trigger — bid/ask widened in final minutes, liquidity evaporated, stop became theoretical. Position expired ITM. Then overnight, SPY continued rallying to ~$676 on Trump unilateral peace announcement. Provisional loss logged at bounded max spread loss ($138,750) pending settlement confirmation. STRUCTURAL LESSONS: (1) End-of-day stops on 0DTE spreads are unreliable — liquidity evaporates precisely when stops are needed. (2) Pin risk near short strike at expiry is unmanageable on mobile. (3) Holding short premium overnight ahead of known binary catalyst is asymmetric. (4) Spread width of 2pt on $0.15 credit means break-even win rate of 92.5% — math too tight. NEW HARD RULES: Flatten all 0DTE positions by 3:30pm ET no exceptions. Never hold short premium overnight ahead of known binary headline events. Minimum 5pt spread width on bear call spreads.
Mistakes: ERROR - Held 0DTE through final minutes when underlying moved into ITM range. Stop did not fire due to end-of-day liquidity evaporation. Held short premium with known overnight binary catalyst pending. Spread width too narrow (2pt) for credit received (0.15) — break-even win rate maths didn't work.
Tags: Bear Call Spread, SPY, 0DTE, Blow-up, Pin-risk, Stop-failure, Catalyst-risk, Largest-loss
